tags.name: analytics
tags.description: Namespace for all routes that are used broadly across vturb, this ultimately delivers the data to the front-end for our clients.

apiToken.name: X-Api-Token
apiToken.description: Access the application and copy your api token, then, just set the header `X-Api-Token` with it.

timed.description: The second of the video that the user reached

timed_conversions.description: The number of conversions of the timed

total_conversions.description: The total of conversions that reached the timed

leaderboard_name.description: Name of the leaderboard (includes the limit)

event.description: Event type that was used to rank the leaderboard

player_id.description: Unique identifier for the player

total_plays.description: Total number of video plays for this player

uniq_plays.description: Number of unique plays (by session) for this player

uniq_device_plays.description: Number of unique device plays for this player

average_watched_time.description: The average time watched by the users in seconds

engagement_rate.description: The engagement rate of the users in percentage, its calculated by the average_watched_time / video_duration * 100

timed.description: The second of the video that the user reached

total_users.description: The total of users that reached the timed

date.description: The date for the engagement rate

engagement_rate.description: The engagement rate for the day

engagement.description: The engagement rate of the users in percentage, its calculated by the average_watched_time / video_duration * 100

play_over_engagement.description: The play over engagement rate of the users in percentage, its calculated by the play_rate / engagement * 100

play_rate.description: The play rate of the users in percentage, its calculated by the views / number * 100

pitch.description: Number of people that went above the pitch time

conversion_rate.description: The conversion rate of the headline

engagement.description: The engagement rate of the users in percentage, its calculated by the average_watched_time / (video_duration * number_of_sessions)

pitch.description: Percentage of people that went above the pitch time

click.description: Percentage of people that clicked on buttons of the page

ComparisonGroupsList.description: A list of AB tests (comparison groups) registered for the authenticated company.

name.description: The name of the comparison group.

player_ids.description: All player ids enrolled in this comparison group.

players.description: Per-player traffic distribution for this comparison group.

traffic_percentage.description: The percentage of traffic routed to this player.

started_at.description: The date/time when traffic started to route to this player. `null` if it hasn't started yet.

locked.description: Whether the player's traffic percentage is locked (won't be auto-adjusted).

started_at.description: The date/time the comparison group test started. `null` if it hasn't started yet.

finished_at.description: The date/time the comparison group test finished. `null` if it is still running.

created_at.description: The date/time when the comparison group was created.

ComparisonGroupsStats.description: Full analytics metrics for the requested players of an AB test.

player_ids.description: Every player id registered in the AB test, even when stats are requested for only a subset.

stats.description: One entry per requested (and valid) player.

pitch_time.description: Pitch time configured for the player, in seconds.

video_duration.description: Duration of the player's video, in seconds.

conversions.description: Conversion totals and revenue in USD, BRL and EUR (major currency units, e.g. `3740.0` = USD $3,740.00).

engagement_rate.description: Percentage of the video that was watched on average (`average_watched_time / video_duration * 100`).

grouped_timed.description: User distribution across watched-time buckets used to derive retention metrics.

total_users.description: Unique users that reached this watched time.

pitch_audience.description: Number of users that watched the video up to (or past) the configured pitch time.

pitch_retention_rate.description: Percentage of users that reached the pitch time (`pitch_audience / total_users * 100`).

play_rate.description: Percentage of viewers that started playing (`plays.total_uniq_device / views.total_uniq_device * 100`).

conversion_rate.description: Percentage of unique plays that converted (`conversions.total_uniq_sessions / plays.total_uniq_device * 100`).

rpv_usd.description: Revenue per unique play in USD (`conversions.total_amount_usd / plays.total_uniq_device`).

rpv_brl.description: Revenue per unique play in BRL.

rpv_eur.description: Revenue per unique play in EUR.

ComparisonGroupsEventTotals.description: Event totals bucketed by total, unique sessions, and unique devices.

interval_seconds.description: Length of this quota window in seconds (e.g. 60 for per-minute, 86400 for per-day).

interval_starts_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp marking the start of the current window.

interval_ends_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp marking when the current window resets.

QuotaMetric.description: One metric within a quota window — used / limit / remaining.

used.description: Amount consumed within the current window.

limit.description: Hard cap for this metric in this window. `null` means no cap.

remaining.description: Budget left (`limit - used`, clamped at 0). `null` when `limit` is `null`.

note.description: Optional explanatory note. Currently set on `queries` when a hard limit applies, to clarify that a single API request may count as more than one query against the limit.

QuotaExceeded.description: 429 body returned when a request exhausts a quota.

error.description: Human-readable error message (stable text, safe to display).

code.description: Upstream error code. `201` indicates quota exceeded.

details.description: Structured fields parsed from the upstream message. Omitted when parsing fails.

interval_seconds.description: Length of the throttling window in seconds.

resets_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the window rolls over and the budget resets.

post.summary: Returns the active platforms for a company
post.description: Returns a list with the company active platforms.

start_date.description: Start date of the period for event querying. This will be used as >=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC" or "2023-10-26"

timezone.description: The timezone to use for the date filtering

400.description: Bad request: the request was bad formatted and some of the arguments were missing or wrong, look at the response body for more information.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

400.description: Bad request: the request was bad formatted and some of the arguments were missing or wrong, look at the response body for more information.

post.summary: Returns the totals of the events for each player in a company
post.description: Returns a list with the companies grouped by its players and the number of times each event happened for each one in a given period.

events.description: Names of the events to filter by. Can be ['started', 'finished', 'viewed']

start_date.description: Start date of the period for event querying. This will be used as >=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC" or "2023-10-26"

end_date.description: End date of the period for event querying. This will be used as <=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC" or "2023-10-26"

player_id.description: The ID of the player

start_date.description: The start date for the player like "2024-01-01"

end_date.description: The end date to filter for the player like "2024-01-01, if none is passed this defaults to tomorrow UTC"

post.summary: Returns player leaderboards based on video engagement metrics
post.description: Provides leaderboard rankings of players based on their video engagement metrics (views, plays, pauses, etc...) within specified time periods. Multiple leaderboards with different player limits can be requested in a single call.

company_id.description: The ID of the company to search for

leaderboard_limit.description: Maximum number of players to include in the leaderboard

start_date.description: Start date of the period for leaderboard data. This will be used as >=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC" or "2023-10-26"

end_date.description: End date of the period for leaderboard data. This will be used as <=. If not provided, will include data up to the current date. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC" or "2023-10-26"

event.description: Event type to be used in the leaderboard ranking

timezone.description: The timezone to use for date calculations (defaults to 'Etc/UCT' if not provided)

400.description: Bad request: the request was bad formatted and some of the arguments were missing or wrong, look at the response body for more information.

401.description: Unauthorized

post.summary: Returns an array with the engagement grouped by a field
post.description: Returns an array containing the overall engagement of the users in a given period for the specified player per day.

player_id.description: The ID of the player to search for

field.description:
The field to group the engagement by, possible values are 'country', 'browser', 'device_type', 'utm_campain', 'utm_source', 'utm_medium', 'utm_content', 'utm_term'
If 'no_attribution' is passed, all values that have been set to null or that are empty strings will be returned.

values.description: The values to filter the field by, for example ['Brazil', 'Romenia'] or ['Chrome', 'Firefox']

start_date.description: Start date of the period for event querying. This will be used as >=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC"

end_date.description: End date of the period for event querying. This will be used as <=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC"

timezone.description: The timezone to use for the date filtering

400.description: Bad request: the request was bad formatted and some of the arguments were missing or wrong, look at the response body for more information.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

post.summary: Statistics used by the headlines dashboard
post.description: Returns several statistics used by the headlines dashboard. Engagement, views and play rate are among these metrics

start_date.description: Start date of the period for event querying.

end_date.description: End date of the period for event querying. Optional — when omitted, the response is unbounded at the upper end. When provided, inclusive at the end of the minute (e.g. `23:59:59` captures the full minute).

player_id.description: The player being analysed.

pitch_time.description: The time in seconds that the video must be watched to be considered a pitch

400.description: If a 400 error occurs, it means parameters were incorrectly passed, and the response body will contain an explanation.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

post.description: Returns statistics of sessions for a player given a date range

player_id.description: The ID of the player to search for

get.summary: Returns the number of live users for a player
get.description: Returns the number of live users for a player that entered the website in the last X minutes. Disclaimer, this doesn't mean the user is still on the website, it means the user entered the website in the last X minutes.

parameters.name: player_id
parameters.description: The ID of the player to search for

parameters.name: minutes
parameters.description: The number of minutes to search for live users, defaults to 60 minutes

live_users.description: The number of live users for the player

400.description: Bad request: the request was bad formatted and some of the arguments were missing or wrong, look at the response body for more information.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

Returns several statistics used by the turbo dashboard.
Speed, engagement, views, pitch and clicks are among these metrics

get.description: Returns a list of all players belonging to the authenticated user's company

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date of the period for player filtering. This will be used as >=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC"

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the period for player filtering. This will be used as <=. Format examples "2023-10-26T18:24:05.000+00:00" or "2023-10-26 18:24:05 UTC"

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: The timezone to use for the date filtering

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter players by name. Search is case-insensitive (including non-ASCII characters such as `É`/`é`). Special characters `%`, `_`, `\`, and brackets are matched literally — for example `name=[campaign_1]` returns only players whose names contain that exact tag. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed before matching; the trimmed value must be between 3 and 128 characters.

parameters.name: name_match
parameters.description: How `name` is matched. `contains` (default) matches anywhere in the name; `starts_with` and `ends_with` anchor to the beginning or end; `exact` requires a full case-insensitive match. Sending `name_match` without `name` returns 400.

id.description: The unique identifier of the player

pitch_time.description: The pitch time configured for the player, if the player doesn't have a pitch time, the value is 0

duration.description: The duration of the video in seconds

created_at.description: The date and time when the player was created

400.description: Bad request: the request was bad formatted and some of the arguments were missing or wrong, look at the response body for more information.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

post.summary: List all custom metrics of a player
post.description: Returns a list of all custom metrics of a player and the calculated engagement rate for them

player_id.description: The player being analysed.

start_date.description: Start date of the period for event querying.

end_date.description: End date of the period for event querying.

timezone.description: The timezone to use for the date filtering, if not provided UTC will be used

id.description: The unique identifier of the custom metric

name.description: The name of the custom metric

time.description: The time in seconds that the custom metric was set

sequential_number.description: The sequential number of the custom metric that ensures ordering

engagement_rate.description: The engagement rate (%) of the custom metric

total_users.description: The total number of users that watched the video

users_above.description: The number of users that watched the video above the pitch time

400.description: If a 400 error occurs, it means parameters were incorrectly passed, and the response body will contain an explanation.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

post.summary: List the AB tests (comparison groups) registered for the authenticated company
post.description: Returns every AB test registered for the company, with the players enrolled in each test (including their traffic percentages) and the test start/finish timestamps. Results are ordered by creation date (newest first). Use the optional `start_date`/`end_date` filters to narrow results by the comparison group `created_at`.

start_date.description: Lower bound applied to the comparison group `created_at`. Format `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. Optional.

end_date.description: Upper bound applied to the comparison group `created_at`. Format `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. Optional — when omitted, no upper bound is applied.

timezone.description: Timezone used to interpret `start_date`/`end_date`. Defaults to `Etc/UTC`.

400.description: Bad request: the request was malformed and some arguments were missing or invalid; see the response body for more information.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

post.summary: Returns the full analytics metrics for up to 2 players of an AB test
post.description: Returns, in a single response, the full set of analytics metrics for up to 2 players of an AB test: views, plays, finishes, clicks, conversions with revenue in USD/BRL/EUR, engagement, pitch audience and pitch retention, as well as the derived play rate, conversion rate and revenue per visitor (RPV). Each item's `start_date` is optional — when omitted, it falls back to the player's own `started_at` (from the comparison group's `players` list) and, if that is not set, to the comparison group's `started_at`. When `end_date` is omitted, results run through the current time.

items.description: Up to 2 players to return stats for. Players not enrolled in the AB test are silently ignored.

player_id.description: The player id.

start_date.description: Inclusive lower bound for the metrics of this player. Format `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. Optional — when omitted, falls back to the player's `started_at` on the comparison group, and finally to the comparison group's own `started_at`.

end_date.description: Inclusive upper bound for the metrics of this player. Format `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. Optional — when omitted, results run through the current time.

events.description: Event names for the `views`/`plays`/`finishes` aggregates. Defaults to `["started", "viewed", "finished"]`.

timezone.description: Timezone used by ClickHouse to interpret every `start_date` / `end_date` string in this request (both caller-provided and the defaults resolved from the comparison group). Defaults to `Etc/UTC`.

400.description: Bad request — malformed parameters or validation errors (e.g. too many items or a malformed `start_date`). The response body carries the details.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

404.description: The `comparison_group_id` does not belong to the authenticated company.

422.description: None of the items referenced a player enrolled in the comparison group.

get.summary: Returns the live API quota usage for the authenticated company
get.description:
Returns the current usage and limits for your API key — one entry per quota window (typically a per-minute and a per-day bucket). Use this endpoint to self-rate-limit before issuing expensive analytics requests.

Notes:
- When a metric has no cap, the response returns `limit: null` and
  `remaining: null` so you don't divide by zero.

- A single API request may count as more than one query against
  `max_queries_per_minute`, so the `queries` counter can climb faster
  than your request rate. The response includes `queries.note` to flag
  this when a hard limit applies. `read_bytes` reflects the actual
  data scanned and is the more reliable signal for sizing usage.

- This endpoint itself counts as 1 query against `max_queries_per_minute`.

401.description: Unauthorized, missing proper X-Api-Token or X-Api-Version

429.description: Quota exceeded. The body carries a stable `error` message, the upstream `code`, and (when parseable) a structured `details` object with the limit kind, remaining budget, and reset timestamp. Other endpoints under this API also use this shape when they hit `code: 201`.
